scenes.description: Opening title card introduces KHK as a familiar fructose enzyme, then reveals the “second job” hook using a clean blue-highlight text treatment.
scenes.framing: Centered title stack on white background; KHK acronym is the visual center with a small liver-cell icon in the upper right.
scenes.movement: Text fades up in two beats: first “fructose enzyme,” then “second job” scales from 0.95 to 1.0.
scenes.overlay_notes: Exact overlay text: “KHK has a second job.” Use Inter bold, blue #2563EB highlight, no more than three colors.
scenes.shot_intent: Create immediate curiosity while keeping the scientific title legible and trustworthy.
scenes.information_role: Viewer learns the video is about a noncanonical KHK role, not a generic fructose explainer.

required_assets.description: Remotion TextCard with exact hook typography and small vector liver-cell icon; no AI-generated text.

scenes.description: Nutrient overload enters a simplified hepatocyte: fructose hexagons and palmitate droplets move toward an ER workspace where unfolded protein shapes begin to accumulate.
scenes.framing: Wide diagrammatic cell cross-section; hepatocyte outline fills center, ER ribbons sit in the middle third, nutrient icons enter from left.
scenes.movement: Fructose and palmitate icons slide in left-to-right; ER protein shapes gently stack up as the narrator says “comes under pressure.”
scenes.overlay_notes: Labels: “fructose,” “saturated fat,” “ER protein-folding workspace.” Keep labels outside foreground depth as overlay badges.
scenes.shot_intent: Translate nutrient stress into a concrete visual load on the ER.
scenes.information_role: Viewer sees why hepatocytes need an ER stress response under fructose-plus-fat overload.

required_assets.description: Flat hepatocyte schematic with fructose and palmitate icons; clean professional blue-white-gray palette.

scenes.description: Progressive pathway reveal: Nutrient overload leads to KHK upregulation, then to adaptive IRE1α-XBP1 signaling and ER chaperone support.
scenes.framing: Full-screen left-to-right pathway diagram with four rounded nodes and generous whitespace.
scenes.movement: Nodes appear sequentially on narration beats; KHK node pulses once as “KHK rises” is spoken; final adaptive branch glows green.
scenes.overlay_notes: Use exact node labels: “Nutrient overload,” “KHK ↑,” “IRE1α-XBP1,” “Adaptive ER support.”
scenes.shot_intent: Show the central protective mechanism as a clear pathway, not a text-heavy pathway dump.
scenes.information_role: Viewer learns that KHK tracks with the adaptive IRE1α-XBP1 arm of UPR.

required_assets.description: Flowchart LR: Nutrient overload --> KHK up --> IRE1α-XBP1 --> Adaptive ER support; GitHub Light/default theme, rounded-node styling, no dense subgraphs.

scenes.description: Evidence triptych showing the same KHK-IRE1α-XBP1 axis across HepG2 cells, GAN diet-fed mouse hepatocytes, and MASLD patient biopsies.
scenes.framing: Three equal cards across the screen; each card has an icon, label, and small upward trend line.
scenes.movement: Cards cascade in one by one: cells, mice, humans. A shared blue line connects the cards to imply conserved axis.
scenes.overlay_notes: Card labels: “HepG2 cells,” “GAN-diet mice,” “MASLD biopsies.” Badge: “conserved axis.”
scenes.shot_intent: Establish that the paper’s signal is not limited to one experimental system.
scenes.information_role: Viewer understands that the axis was observed in cell, mouse, and human contexts.

required_assets.description: Three evidence cards with icons and mini trend arrows; exact labels rendered in Remotion.

scenes.description: Hero split-screen twist: the adaptive XBP1 route dims while misfolded proteins, ubiquitin tags, and PERK-CHOP apoptotic signaling intensify after KHK knockdown.
scenes.framing: Split screen with left adaptive branch in blue/green and right stress/apoptosis branch in amber/red; KHK knockdown toggle sits at center.
scenes.movement: Center toggle flips to “KHK knockdown”; left branch opacity lowers; right branch aggregates multiply and red warning line grows toward apoptosis.
scenes.shot_intent: Deliver the surprising reversal that makes the paper memorable.
scenes.information_role: Viewer learns that removing KHK disrupts proteostasis and shifts cells toward apoptotic UPR.

required_assets.description: Animated split pathway: adaptive XBP1 support dims; PERK-CHOP apoptotic branch intensifies with aggregate icons and ubiquitin tags.

scenes.description: Dual-role KHK card: KHK in the center with two labeled functions—fructose flux gatekeeper and ER proteostasis safeguard.
scenes.framing: Centered KHK node with two balanced side panels; left panel metabolic flux, right panel ER protection.
scenes.movement: KHK node appears first; left and right role labels expand outward; a subtle checkmark appears on “safeguard.”
scenes.overlay_notes: Exact role labels: “fructose flux gatekeeper” and “ER proteostasis safeguard.”
scenes.shot_intent: Turn the mechanistic findings into a memorable two-role takeaway.
scenes.information_role: Viewer remembers KHK as a dual-function protein, not just a fructose enzyme.

required_assets.description: Simple central KHK node branching to two function nodes: fructose flux gatekeeper and ER proteostasis safeguard; sparse labels only.

scenes.description: Therapeutic precision message: reduce harmful fructose flux without losing ER protection, framed as a balanced translational takeaway.
scenes.framing: Large centered sentence with “precision” highlighted; small ER shield icon on the right.
scenes.movement: Keyword “precision” fades in amber; phrase “without losing ER protection” underlines in green.
scenes.overlay_notes: Exact overlay: “Precision: reduce fructose flux without losing ER protection.”
scenes.shot_intent: Land the translational implication without overstating clinical advice.
scenes.information_role: Viewer understands the therapeutic nuance: selective inhibition may matter.

required_assets.description: Exact-text Remotion card for therapeutic precision message; no generated image text.

scenes.description: Final DOI and paper card with title, journal, and DOI shown exactly for sharing and follow-up.
scenes.framing: Centered citation card; title wraps in two lines, DOI in blue button-style badge at bottom.
scenes.movement: Citation card fades in; DOI badge gently pulses once to cue screenshot/share.
scenes.overlay_notes: Exact text: “Beyond fructolysis: ketohexokinase orchestrates ER proteostasis in nutrient-stressed hepatocytes” and DOI “10.1152/ajpgi.00235.2025.”
scenes.shot_intent: Provide a readable, accurate citation and call to read the open-access paper.
scenes.information_role: Viewer knows where to find the paper.

required_assets.description: Exact citation card rendered by Remotion to avoid hallucinated DOI/title text.

metadata.coverage: Scenes cover script sections s1-s7 with no timeline gaps.
metadata.visual_strategy: Remotion-native text cards and animations plus Mermaid-compatible diagrams; no unavailable image/video generation required.

summary.success_criteria_met: Full 63-second duration covered, all script sections represented, three scene types used, and all assets are feasible with Remotion/Mermaid path.

metadata.awaiting: scene plan approval before asset generation
